title: Paths, Navigation, and Filesystem Hierarchy · Independent Operations Trial
summary: Locate application configuration and logs on a workstation whose current directory and distribution conventions differ from a familiar machine. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.
Use pwd, cd, and directory stacks while predicting the resulting working directory before each transition.
Relate root-level directories to boot, configuration, devices, variable data, user data, runtime state, and optional software roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.
